perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) A male host in a grey t-shirt, black cap, and denim shorts stands in an indoor waiting area, holding a black book with a gold logo. (2) The scene cuts to close-up shots of a stamp book being flipped through, showing various decorative stamps and calligraphy with airport names like Tokyo and Fukuoka. (3) The host gestures toward a 'JAL PLAZA' shop and an information desk inside an airport terminal. (4) The host stands outside under a blue sky with flags flying at Yamaguchi Ube Airport, then shows himself inside at a check-in counter and a convenience store. (5) The video ends with a montage of the host holding up specific stamps at Kitakyushu Airport and giving a thumbs up.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, energetic electronic pop music begins with a consistent drum beat. A male voice speaks enthusiastically in Mandarin. (2) Background noise of a public space (echoing voices, footsteps) is heard as the music continues at a steady volume. (3) High-pitched notification sound effects ('ping') and digital 'swoosh' sounds occur during visual transitions. (4) The music remains constant as the male host continues his narration with consistent energy and speed.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) '來 今天嘎哥跟你分享' (Come, today Gaga shares with you) - Hook. (2) '日本旅遊除了御朱印 還可以蒐集什麼?' (Besides Goshuin, what else can you collect while traveling in Japan?) - Informational hook. (3) '御翔印' (Gyoshon-in) - Main subject title. (4) '日本各大機場 才有在賣的' (Only sold at major airports in Japan) - Detail. (5) '山口宇部空港 GET' (Yamaguchi Ube Airport GET) - Achievement text. (6) '御翔印 最難收的日本紀念品 歡迎入坑' (Gyoshon-in, the hardest-to-collect Japanese souvenir, welcome to the hobby) - Closing hook.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro -> Concept Explanation -> Practical Demonstration (Yamaguchi Airport) -> Practical Demonstration (Kitakyushu Airport) -> Conclusion/CTA

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main host creator, approximately 4 passers-by visible in various airport shots.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A male with short brown hair wearing a black baseball cap, an oversized grey t-shirt, dark denim shorts, black socks, brown sneakers, and a black 'MATCHWOOD' crossbody bag.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Interiors of modern Japanese airports including check-in counters (JAL), waiting lounges with brown seats, retail shops (Yamazaki, JAL Plaza), and exterior shots of airport buildings with Japanese and regional flags.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Mixed: Bright fluorescent indoor lighting and clear, direct natural sunlight outdoors.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Introduction of Item -> Sourcing Locations -> Personal Demonstration -> Summary/Closing
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to educate viewers on a specific Japanese collectible called 'Gyoshon-in' (airport stamps), providing logistical details on how to find them at different Japanese airports (JAL shops) and highlighting the challenge and satisfaction of collecting them as a unique travel souvenir.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Frequent display of the JAL (Japan Airlines) logo and the specific product line of stamps which are a JAL commercial initiative.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Host refers to himself as 'Gaga' (嘎哥), high-energy presentation style, fast-paced 'beat-matching' edits.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively targets travel enthusiasts and 'stamp' collectors by transforming a travel logistics video into a 'quest' or 'collection challenge' narrative.
